title: calystegia spithamaea subsp. spithamaea
intro: Calystegia spithamaea subsp. spithamaea, also known as small bindweed or wild morning glory, is a perennial climbing plant native to North America, characterized by its small white flowers and twining stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a natural antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant, making it useful in treating conditions such as asthma, bronchitis, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Calystegia spithamaea subsp. spithamaea include reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and promoting digestive health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being dried and used in herbal blends, with some also using it as a smoking herb for its medicinal benefits.
